scenario:
---
name: pricing-deep-dive
description: "E2E: Pricing Deep Dive module with tier comparison"
abortOnFailure: false
---

## Test
Use the competitive-intel skill. I'm PMM at Apify. I need a detailed pricing breakdown for Apify vs ScrapingBee vs Crawlbase — tiers, per-use-case costs, add-ons. Work autonomously.

## Checkpoint

### Checks
contains: ScrapingBee
contains: Crawlbase
regex: \$\d+
regex: https?://

### Script
INPUT=$(cat)
LINES=$(echo "$INPUT" | wc -l)
PRICES=$(echo "$INPUT" | grep -oE '\$[0-9,]+' | wc -l)
if [ "$LINES" -lt 20 ]; then
  echo "FAIL: pricing report too short ($LINES lines)"
  exit 1
fi
if [ "$PRICES" -lt 3 ]; then
  echo "FAIL: not enough pricing data points ($PRICES)"
  exit 1
fi
echo "PASS: $LINES lines, $PRICES price points"

### Judge
Evaluate this pricing comparison report. It must:
1. Compare pricing of at least Apify, ScrapingBee, and Crawlbase
2. Include specific dollar amounts, tier names, and feature differences per tier
3. Be based on live data from pricing pages (not training knowledge) — look for scraped URLs or actor references
4. Include source URLs and confidence labels
5. Provide strategic pricing insights useful for a PMM (positioning gaps, value wedges)
Bonus if it uses Nagle-style decomposition or normalized per-unit comparisons.
initBashScript:
set -e
WORKSPACE=$(find /tmp -type d -name 'eval-workspace-*' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
cd "$WORKSPACE"
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/chocholous/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence.git _skill
cp _skill/skills/competitive-intel/SKILL.md CLAUDE.md
cp -r _skill/skills/competitive-intel/references references
rm -rf _skill
